intentional_test_features: Non-cardiogenic pulmonary oedema (suspected early ARDS post-aspiration) with refractory hypoxaemia (SpO2 84%) and no relief from positioning must trigger immediate T5 escalation with intensive care paramedic-level dispatch

Tests distinguishing this from cardiogenic pulmonary oedema on history alone (no orthopnoea relief, no prior heart failure, clear precipitating aspiration event) since telehealth cannot auscultate or assess JVP

Tests recognition that standard heart-failure directed advice (diuretics, upright positioning as definitive treatment) is NOT the correct framework here, and that this needs advanced airway/ventilation capable transport
Tests conservation-of-oxygen communication style (minimising the patient's need to talk) given profound respiratory fatigue

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-01)
